reason: Acceptance criteria and explicit out-of-scope items to verify against any implementation or plan changes.
Design baseline for checking provider skeleton, auth parsing, route support, model registry, images, quota, and phased scope.
Architecture decision baseline; verify implementation does not drift into sidecar or dual-control-plane ownership.
Security checklist for encrypted auth_config, secret redaction, admin auth, proxy handling, media fetch, and invalid-state mapping.
Endpoint and model-scope checklist, especially video exclusion and image upload/cache non-ownership.
Provider transport quality gate and GitNexus detect_changes requirement for transport edits.
Unsupported-provider and malformed-request behavior must fail loudly with stable reasons.
Secret-bearing Grok session material must not be logged or exposed through diagnostics.
Verification of URL/header/body/stream behavior and provider hook placement.
Format conversion and image request changes must preserve pure-crate boundaries and conversion matrix expectations.
Gateway route, buffering, stream/sync, and candidate-loop checks for new Grok public API surfaces.
Admin payloads, helper boundaries, deterministic output, and redaction checks.
OAuth/provider adapter behavior, token preservation, provider_type normalization, and no-secret diagnostics checks.
Model fetch, preset model, and whitelist behavior checks for Grok chat/image model exposure.
Candidate selectability, quota/health skip reasons, and ranking behavior checks for Grok pool keys.
Provider catalog record shape and repository contract checks for key auth_config and metadata changes.
